vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大穴持神社'''（おおなむぢじんじゃ[https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html 大穴持神社]（鹿児島県神社庁）。）は、{{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|霧島市|Q858352}}国分広瀬にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。神紋は「五七の桐」。通称を「オナンジサア」{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の通り境内説明板（大穴持神社設置）。境内説明板（霧島市教育委員会設置）。。 ; 主祭神 :* '''{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大巳貴命}}'''（おおなんじのみこと/おおなむちのみこと、大国主命） ; 相殿神 :* {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}}（すくなひこのみこと/すくなびこなのみこと） :* {{jalink|大年神||大歳神}}（おおとしがみ、大歳命） :* {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}} :* 霧島大神 - {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}22年（{{jalink|1947年|Q5263}}）に現在の広瀬公民館の地から遷座・合祀。 祭神の人格神をオオナムチ（オオクニヌシ）に比定する説は、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}9年（{{jalink|778年|Q30452}}）に遡り、『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}でも「大穴持神社」と見える。出雲地方を起点とするオオクニヌシ信仰が鹿児島まで及ぶことを示す神社として紹介されることもある[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHKスペシャル「シリーズ遷宮 第2回 出雲大社 ～オオクニヌシの謎～」] NHK、2014年1月2日初回放送。。現在は医療の神・まむし除けの神として信仰される。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 創建は不詳。社記では{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}年間（{{jalink|729年|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749年|Q23624}}）の創建とする{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。元は後述の神造島（宮洲、宮世洲）に鎮座したが、島崩れにより現在地に遷座したとする伝承がある。 『国分諸古記』・『神社仏閣帳』では、当初は奥州津軽山に鎮座したとし、{{jalink|日向国|Q513737}}串島、{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002}}福瀬之渡、福島村を経て小村の当地に鎮座したとする伝承を記す{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』では、古代の大穴持神社に関して次のように見える{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 * {{jalink|天平宝字|Q1346307}}8年（{{jalink|764年|Q31163}}）12月是月条 - 大隅国・{{jalink|薩摩国|Q667475}}境で起こった噴火によって3島が出現。 * {{jalink|天平神護|Q1373181}}2年（{{jalink|766年|Q30564}}）6月5日条 - 「大隅国神」が新島を造り震動がやまず、人民の多くが流亡したので、物をめぐみ救済した。 * {{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}9年（{{jalink|778年|Q30452}}）12月12日条 - 神護年間に島を造った神の名は「大穴持神」であり、官社とした。 出現した3島については、隼人港沖の辺田小島・弁天島・沖小島に比定する説が一般的であった{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。しかし火山学的にはそれらは奈良時代の噴火で生じたものではないとされ、実際の3島は{{jalink|桜島|Q128841}}付近に所在したがその後に海没したとする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 またこの神造島（宮洲、宮世洲）は、天降川の沖左側に宮瀬と呼ばれる瀬があるので、そこが水没して現在の瀬になったのではないかともいわれている。 この他にも『'''八幡宇佐宮御託宣集'''』には、火山活動で大隅の海中にできた島を{{jalink|神護景雲|Q1373049}}2年（{{jalink|768年|Q29961}}）に「鹿児島」と名付けるとの記述がある。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大隅国{{jalink|曽於郡|Q1043870|囎唹郡}}に「大穴持神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 {{jalink|中世|Q12554}}期について、{{jalink|建治|Q1327993}}2年（{{jalink|1276年|Q5530}}）の「石築地役配符写」では「大穴持新田五反」と記載があり、{{jalink|御家人|Q63765}}の「姫□太夫篤季（□は脱字）」が神主大宮司を担ったと見える{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}期には祭米として3斗3升3合{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}（または3斗5升5合{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}）が給されており、その頃から現在まで谷口家が社家を担っている{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。また{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}14年（{{jalink|1843年|Q7629}}）の『{{jalink|三国名勝図会|Q11354726}}』には当時の境内の様子が描かれており、当時は鳥居前に海が広がっていたことが知られる。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|県社}}に列している{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 境内 === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|拝殿 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|鳥居 === 摂末社 === 境内末社として次の5社がある。 * 日天宮 * 稲荷宮 * 月天宮 * 大田宮 * 大王宮 これら5社は『三国名勝図会』にも描かれ、現在とほぼ同位置に鎮座する。 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 === 祭事 === {{hidden begin |title = 年間祭事一覧 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} 大穴持神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 毎朝 ** 日供祭 * 毎月 ** 月次祭（毎月1日） * 1月 ** 歳旦祭（1月1日） * 2月 ** 建国記念日（2月11日） * 3月 ** 祈年祭（旧暦2月13日） ** 春分祭（春分日） * 4月 ** まむし祓除祭（旧暦3月13日） * 6月 ** 大祓（6月30日） * 7月 ** 神幸祭（7月吉日） ** 六月燈祭（7月吉日） * 9月 ** 秋分祭（秋分日） ** '''例大祭'''（9月29日） * 11月 ** 霧島大祭（11月19日） ** 勤労感謝祭（11月23日） * 12月 ** 霜月丑祭（旧11月丑日） ** 天長祭（12月23日） ** 除夜祭（大祓）（12月31日） {{hidden end}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=大穴持神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/855}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）855-856コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=藤井重壽|chapter=大穴持神社|year=1978|title=式内社調査報告 第24巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=大穴持神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 47 鹿児島県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910548|ref={{Harvid|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} * {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 大穴持神社 |画像 = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|霧島市|Q858352}}国分広瀬3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=大穴持神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大巳貴命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = {{jalink|9月29日|Q2882}} |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=大穴持神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大穴持神社'''（おおなむぢじんじゃ[https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html 大穴持神社]（鹿児島県神社庁）。）は、{{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|霧島市|Q858352}}国分広瀬にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。神紋は「五七の桐」。通称を「オナンジサア」{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の通り境内説明板（大穴持神社設置）。境内説明板（霧島市教育委員会設置）。。 ; 主祭神 :* '''{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大巳貴命}}'''（おおなんじのみこと/おおなむちのみこと、大国主命） ; 相殿神 :* {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}}（すくなひこのみこと/すくなびこなのみこと） :* {{jalink|大年神||大歳神}}（おおとしがみ、大歳命） :* {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}} :* 霧島大神 - {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}22年（{{jalink|1947年|Q5263}}）に現在の広瀬公民館の地から遷座・合祀。 祭神の人格神をオオナムチ（オオクニヌシ）に比定する説は、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}9年（{{jalink|778年|Q30452}}）に遡り、『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}でも「大穴持神社」と見える。出雲地方を起点とするオオクニヌシ信仰が鹿児島まで及ぶことを示す神社として紹介されることもある[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHKスペシャル「シリーズ遷宮 第2回 出雲大社 ～オオクニヌシの謎～」] NHK、2014年1月2日初回放送。。現在は医療の神・まむし除けの神として信仰される。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 創建は不詳。社記では{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}年間（{{jalink|729年|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749年|Q23624}}）の創建とする{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。元は後述の神造島（宮洲、宮世洲）に鎮座したが、島崩れにより現在地に遷座したとする伝承がある。 『国分諸古記』・『神社仏閣帳』では、当初は奥州津軽山に鎮座したとし、{{jalink|日向国|Q513737}}串島、{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002}}福瀬之渡、福島村を経て小村の当地に鎮座したとする伝承を記す{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』では、古代の大穴持神社に関して次のように見える{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 * {{jalink|天平宝字|Q1346307}}8年（{{jalink|764年|Q31163}}）12月是月条 - 大隅国・{{jalink|薩摩国|Q667475}}境で起こった噴火によって3島が出現。 * {{jalink|天平神護|Q1373181}}2年（{{jalink|766年|Q30564}}）6月5日条 - 「大隅国神」が新島を造り震動がやまず、人民の多くが流亡したので、物をめぐみ救済した。 * {{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}9年（{{jalink|778年|Q30452}}）12月12日条 - 神護年間に島を造った神の名は「大穴持神」であり、官社とした。 出現した3島については、隼人港沖の辺田小島・弁天島・沖小島に比定する説が一般的であった{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。しかし火山学的にはそれらは奈良時代の噴火で生じたものではないとされ、実際の3島は{{jalink|桜島|Q128841}}付近に所在したがその後に海没したとする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 またこの神造島（宮洲、宮世洲）は、天降川の沖左側に宮瀬と呼ばれる瀬があるので、そこが水没して現在の瀬になったのではないかともいわれている。 この他にも『'''八幡宇佐宮御託宣集'''』には、火山活動で大隅の海中にできた島を{{jalink|神護景雲|Q1373049}}2年（{{jalink|768年|Q29961}}）に「鹿児島」と名付けるとの記述がある。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大隅国{{jalink|曽於郡|Q1043870|囎唹郡}}に「大穴持神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 {{jalink|中世|Q12554}}期について、{{jalink|建治|Q1327993}}2年（{{jalink|1276年|Q5530}}）の「石築地役配符写」では「大穴持新田五反」と記載があり、{{jalink|御家人|Q63765}}の「姫□太夫篤季（□は脱字）」が神主大宮司を担ったと見える{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}期には祭米として3斗3升3合{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}（または3斗5升5合{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}）が給されており、その頃から現在まで谷口家が社家を担っている{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。また{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}14年（{{jalink|1843年|Q7629}}）の『{{jalink|三国名勝図会|Q11354726}}』には当時の境内の様子が描かれており、当時は鳥居前に海が広がっていたことが知られる。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|県社}}に列している{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 境内 === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|拝殿 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|鳥居 === 摂末社 === 境内末社として次の5社がある。 * 日天宮 * 稲荷宮 * 月天宮 * 大田宮 * 大王宮 これら5社は『三国名勝図会』にも描かれ、現在とほぼ同位置に鎮座する。 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 === 祭事 === {{hidden begin |title = 年間祭事一覧 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} 大穴持神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 毎朝 ** 日供祭 * 毎月 ** 月次祭（毎月1日） * 1月 ** 歳旦祭（1月1日） * 2月 ** 建国記念日（2月11日） * 3月 ** 祈年祭（旧暦2月13日） ** 春分祭（春分日） * 4月 ** まむし祓除祭（旧暦3月13日） * 6月 ** 大祓（6月30日） * 7月 ** 神幸祭（7月吉日） ** 六月燈祭（7月吉日） * 9月 ** 秋分祭（秋分日） ** '''例大祭'''（9月29日） * 11月 ** 霧島大祭（11月19日） ** 勤労感謝祭（11月23日） * 12月 ** 霜月丑祭（旧11月丑日） ** 天長祭（12月23日） ** 除夜祭（大祓）（12月31日） {{hidden end}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} category (P373) == * Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '鹿児島県霧島市国分広瀬3-1089', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} [[JA:大穴持神社 (霧島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 大穴持神社 |画像 = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|霧島市|Q858352}}国分広瀬3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=大穴持神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大巳貴命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = {{jalink|9月29日|Q2882}} |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=大穴持神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大穴持神社'''（おおなむぢじんじゃ[https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html 大穴持神社]（鹿児島県神社庁）。）は、{{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|霧島市|Q858352}}国分広瀬にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。神紋は「五七の桐」。通称を「オナンジサア」{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の通り境内説明板（大穴持神社設置）。境内説明板（霧島市教育委員会設置）。。 ; 主祭神 :* '''{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大巳貴命}}'''（おおなんじのみこと/おおなむちのみこと、大国主命） ; 相殿神 :* {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}}（すくなひこのみこと/すくなびこなのみこと） :* {{jalink|大年神||大歳神}}（おおとしがみ、大歳命） :* {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}} :* 霧島大神 - {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}22年（{{jalink|1947年|Q5263}}）に現在の広瀬公民館の地から遷座・合祀。 祭神の人格神をオオナムチ（オオクニヌシ）に比定する説は、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}9年（{{jalink|778年|Q30452}}）に遡り、『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}でも「大穴持神社」と見える。出雲地方を起点とするオオクニヌシ信仰が鹿児島まで及ぶことを示す神社として紹介されることもある[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHKスペシャル「シリーズ遷宮 第2回 出雲大社 ～オオクニヌシの謎～」] NHK、2014年1月2日初回放送。。現在は医療の神・まむし除けの神として信仰される。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 創建は不詳。社記では{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}年間（{{jalink|729年|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749年|Q23624}}）の創建とする{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。元は後述の神造島（宮洲、宮世洲）に鎮座したが、島崩れにより現在地に遷座したとする伝承がある。 『国分諸古記』・『神社仏閣帳』では、当初は奥州津軽山に鎮座したとし、{{jalink|日向国|Q513737}}串島、{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002}}福瀬之渡、福島村を経て小村の当地に鎮座したとする伝承を記す{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』では、古代の大穴持神社に関して次のように見える{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 * {{jalink|天平宝字|Q1346307}}8年（{{jalink|764年|Q31163}}）12月是月条 - 大隅国・{{jalink|薩摩国|Q667475}}境で起こった噴火によって3島が出現。 * {{jalink|天平神護|Q1373181}}2年（{{jalink|766年|Q30564}}）6月5日条 - 「大隅国神」が新島を造り震動がやまず、人民の多くが流亡したので、物をめぐみ救済した。 * {{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}9年（{{jalink|778年|Q30452}}）12月12日条 - 神護年間に島を造った神の名は「大穴持神」であり、官社とした。 出現した3島については、隼人港沖の辺田小島・弁天島・沖小島に比定する説が一般的であった{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。しかし火山学的にはそれらは奈良時代の噴火で生じたものではないとされ、実際の3島は{{jalink|桜島|Q128841}}付近に所在したがその後に海没したとする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 またこの神造島（宮洲、宮世洲）は、天降川の沖左側に宮瀬と呼ばれる瀬があるので、そこが水没して現在の瀬になったのではないかともいわれている。 この他にも『'''八幡宇佐宮御託宣集'''』には、火山活動で大隅の海中にできた島を{{jalink|神護景雲|Q1373049}}2年（{{jalink|768年|Q29961}}）に「鹿児島」と名付けるとの記述がある。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大隅国{{jalink|曽於郡|Q1043870|囎唹郡}}に「大穴持神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 {{jalink|中世|Q12554}}期について、{{jalink|建治|Q1327993}}2年（{{jalink|1276年|Q5530}}）の「石築地役配符写」では「大穴持新田五反」と記載があり、{{jalink|御家人|Q63765}}の「姫□太夫篤季（□は脱字）」が神主大宮司を担ったと見える{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}期には祭米として3斗3升3合{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}（または3斗5升5合{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}）が給されており、その頃から現在まで谷口家が社家を担っている{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。また{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}14年（{{jalink|1843年|Q7629}}）の『{{jalink|三国名勝図会|Q11354726}}』には当時の境内の様子が描かれており、当時は鳥居前に海が広がっていたことが知られる。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|県社}}に列している{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 境内 === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|拝殿 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|鳥居 === 摂末社 === 境内末社として次の5社がある。 * 日天宮 * 稲荷宮 * 月天宮 * 大田宮 * 大王宮 これら5社は『三国名勝図会』にも描かれ、現在とほぼ同位置に鎮座する。 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 === 祭事 === {{hidden begin |title = 年間祭事一覧 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} 大穴持神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 毎朝 ** 日供祭 * 毎月 ** 月次祭（毎月1日） * 1月 ** 歳旦祭（1月1日） * 2月 ** 建国記念日（2月11日） * 3月 ** 祈年祭（旧暦2月13日） ** 春分祭（春分日） * 4月 ** まむし祓除祭（旧暦3月13日） * 6月 ** 大祓（6月30日） * 7月 ** 神幸祭（7月吉日） ** 六月燈祭（7月吉日） * 9月 ** 秋分祭（秋分日） ** '''例大祭'''（9月29日） * 11月 ** 霧島大祭（11月19日） ** 勤労感謝祭（11月23日） * 12月 ** 霜月丑祭（旧11月丑日） ** 天長祭（12月23日） ** 除夜祭（大祓）（12月31日） {{hidden end}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} Torii and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima|Q858352}} Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōmunaji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|September 29|Q2882}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Ōmunaji Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmunaji Shrine''' (おおなむぢじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Kirishima, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha and a former prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition places its origin in the Tenpyō era. It is said to have originally stood on a now-lost island (Kamizō-jima / Miyosezu) and later moved to its present location. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is listed in Soo District, Ōsumi Province as “Ōanamochi Shrine.” === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html Ōanamochi Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなむちしんしや きりしまし}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōsumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:霧島市の歴史]] [[Category:霧島市の建築物]] [[Category:大隅国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōmunaji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima|Q858352}} Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōmunaji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|September 29|Q2882}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Ōmunaji Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmunaji Shrine''' (おおなむぢじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Kirishima, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha and a former prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition places its origin in the Tenpyō era. It is said to have originally stood on a now-lost island (Kamizō-jima / Miyosezu) and later moved to its present location. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is listed in Soo District, Ōsumi Province as “Ōanamochi Shrine.” === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html Ōanamochi Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなむちしんしや きりしまし}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōsumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:霧島市の歴史]] [[Category:霧島市の建築物]] [[Category:大隅国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

{{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōmunaji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima|Q858352}} Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōmunaji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|September 29|Q2882}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Ōmunaji Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmunaji Shrine''' (おおなむぢじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Kirishima, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha and a former prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition places its origin in the Tenpyō era. It is said to have originally stood on a now-lost island (Kamizō-jima / Miyosezu) and later moved to its present location. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is listed in Soo District, Ōsumi Province as “Ōanamochi Shrine.” === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html Ōanamochi Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなむちしんしや きりしまし}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōsumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:霧島市の歴史]] [[Category:霧島市の建築物]] [[Category:大隅国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōmunaji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima|Q858352}} Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōmunaji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|September 29|Q2882}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Ōmunaji Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmunaji Shrine''' (おおなむぢじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Kirishima, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha and a former prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition places its origin in the Tenpyō era. It is said to have originally stood on a now-lost island (Kamizō-jima / Miyosezu) and later moved to its present location. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is listed in Soo District, Ōsumi Province as “Ōanamochi Shrine.” === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html Ōanamochi Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなむちしんしや きりしまし}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōsumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:霧島市の歴史]] [[Category:霧島市の建築物]] [[Category:大隅国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|September 29|Q2882}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Ōmunaji Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmunaji Shrine''' (おおなむぢじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Kirishima, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha and a former prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition places its origin in the Tenpyō era. It is said to have originally stood on a now-lost island (Kamizō-jima / Miyosezu) and later moved to its present location. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is listed in Soo District, Ōsumi Province as “Ōanamochi Shrine.” === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html Ōanamochi Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなむちしんしや きりしまし}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōsumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:霧島市の歴史]] [[Category:霧島市の建築物]] [[Category:大隅国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]]

{{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōmunaji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima|Q858352}} Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōmunaji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|September 29|Q2882}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Ōmunaji Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmunaji Shrine''' (おおなむぢじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Kirishima, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha and a former prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition places its origin in the Tenpyō era. It is said to have originally stood on a now-lost island (Kamizō-jima / Miyosezu) and later moved to its present location. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is listed in Soo District, Ōsumi Province as “Ōanamochi Shrine.” === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html Ōanamochi Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなむちしんしや きりしまし}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōsumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:霧島市の歴史]] [[Category:霧島市の建築物]] [[Category:大隅国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ōmunaji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima|Q858352}} Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōmunaji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |例祭 = {{jalink|September 29|Q2882}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Ōmunaji Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmunaji Shrine''' (おおなむぢじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Kirishima, Kagoshima Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha and a former prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition places its origin in the Tenpyō era. It is said to have originally stood on a now-lost island (Kamizō-jima / Miyosezu) and later moved to its present location. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it is listed in Soo District, Ōsumi Province as “Ōanamochi Shrine.” === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main sanctuary File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Worship hall File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html Ōanamochi Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおなむちしんしや きりしまし}} [[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmunaji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōsumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:霧島市の歴史]] [[Category:霧島市の建築物]] [[Category:大隅国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 大穴持神社 |画像 = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|霧島市|Q858352}}国分広瀬3-1089 |位置 = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=大穴持神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大巳貴命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = {{jalink|9月29日|Q2882}} |本殿 = |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=大穴持神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大穴持神社'''（おおなむぢじんじゃ[https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-203.html 大穴持神社]（鹿児島県神社庁）。）は、{{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|霧島市|Q858352}}国分広瀬にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。神紋は「五七の桐」。通称を「オナンジサア」{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の通り境内説明板（大穴持神社設置）。境内説明板（霧島市教育委員会設置）。。 ; 主祭神 :* '''{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大巳貴命}}'''（おおなんじのみこと/おおなむちのみこと、大国主命） ; 相殿神 :* {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}}（すくなひこのみこと/すくなびこなのみこと） :* {{jalink|大年神||大歳神}}（おおとしがみ、大歳命） :* {{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}} :* 霧島大神 - {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}22年（{{jalink|1947年|Q5263}}）に現在の広瀬公民館の地から遷座・合祀。 祭神の人格神をオオナムチ（オオクニヌシ）に比定する説は、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}9年（{{jalink|778年|Q30452}}）に遡り、『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}でも「大穴持神社」と見える。出雲地方を起点とするオオクニヌシ信仰が鹿児島まで及ぶことを示す神社として紹介されることもある[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHKスペシャル「シリーズ遷宮 第2回 出雲大社 ～オオクニヌシの謎～」] NHK、2014年1月2日初回放送。。現在は医療の神・まむし除けの神として信仰される。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 創建は不詳。社記では{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}年間（{{jalink|729年|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749年|Q23624}}）の創建とする{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。元は後述の神造島（宮洲、宮世洲）に鎮座したが、島崩れにより現在地に遷座したとする伝承がある。 『国分諸古記』・『神社仏閣帳』では、当初は奥州津軽山に鎮座したとし、{{jalink|日向国|Q513737}}串島、{{jalink|大隅国|Q981002}}福瀬之渡、福島村を経て小村の当地に鎮座したとする伝承を記す{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』では、古代の大穴持神社に関して次のように見える{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 * {{jalink|天平宝字|Q1346307}}8年（{{jalink|764年|Q31163}}）12月是月条 - 大隅国・{{jalink|薩摩国|Q667475}}境で起こった噴火によって3島が出現。 * {{jalink|天平神護|Q1373181}}2年（{{jalink|766年|Q30564}}）6月5日条 - 「大隅国神」が新島を造り震動がやまず、人民の多くが流亡したので、物をめぐみ救済した。 * {{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}9年（{{jalink|778年|Q30452}}）12月12日条 - 神護年間に島を造った神の名は「大穴持神」であり、官社とした。 出現した3島については、隼人港沖の辺田小島・弁天島・沖小島に比定する説が一般的であった{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。しかし火山学的にはそれらは奈良時代の噴火で生じたものではないとされ、実際の3島は{{jalink|桜島|Q128841}}付近に所在したがその後に海没したとする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 またこの神造島（宮洲、宮世洲）は、天降川の沖左側に宮瀬と呼ばれる瀬があるので、そこが水没して現在の瀬になったのではないかともいわれている。 この他にも『'''八幡宇佐宮御託宣集'''』には、火山活動で大隅の海中にできた島を{{jalink|神護景雲|Q1373049}}2年（{{jalink|768年|Q29961}}）に「鹿児島」と名付けるとの記述がある。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では大隅国{{jalink|曽於郡|Q1043870|囎唹郡}}に「大穴持神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 {{jalink|中世|Q12554}}期について、{{jalink|建治|Q1327993}}2年（{{jalink|1276年|Q5530}}）の「石築地役配符写」では「大穴持新田五反」と記載があり、{{jalink|御家人|Q63765}}の「姫□太夫篤季（□は脱字）」が神主大宮司を担ったと見える{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}期には祭米として3斗3升3合{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}（または3斗5升5合{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}）が給されており、その頃から現在まで谷口家が社家を担っている{{Sfn|大穴持神社（平凡社）|1998}}。また{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}14年（{{jalink|1843年|Q7629}}）の『{{jalink|三国名勝図会|Q11354726}}』には当時の境内の様子が描かれており、当時は鳥居前に海が広がっていたことが知られる。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|県社}}に列している{{Sfn|大穴持神社（式内社）|1978}}。 === 境内 === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|拝殿 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|鳥居 === 摂末社 === 境内末社として次の5社がある。 * 日天宮 * 稲荷宮 * 月天宮 * 大田宮 * 大王宮 これら5社は『三国名勝図会』にも描かれ、現在とほぼ同位置に鎮座する。 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 === 祭事 === {{hidden begin |title = 年間祭事一覧 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} 大穴持神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 毎朝 ** 日供祭 * 毎月 ** 月次祭（毎月1日） * 1月 ** 歳旦祭（1月1日） * 2月 ** 建国記念日（2月11日） * 3月 ** 祈年祭（旧暦2月13日） ** 春分祭（春分日） * 4月 ** まむし祓除祭（旧暦3月13日） * 6月 ** 大祓（6月30日） * 7月 ** 神幸祭（7月吉日） ** 六月燈祭（7月吉日） * 9月 ** 秋分祭（秋分日） ** '''例大祭'''（9月29日） * 11月 ** 霧島大祭（11月19日） ** 勤労感謝祭（11月23日） * 12月 ** 霜月丑祭（旧11月丑日） ** 天長祭（12月23日） ** 除夜祭（大祓）（12月31日） {{hidden end}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{jalink|September 29th|Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Ootoshigami||Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}22 ({{jalink|1947|Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{jalink|Houki|Q18520676}} of {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}} ({{jalink| It dates back to 778 | Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} years ({{jalink|729|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749|Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}} Kushijima, {{jalink| Osumi Province|Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307}} December 8 ({{jalink|764|Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{jalink|766|Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{jalink|Hoki|Q18520676}}9th year ({{jalink|778th year|Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{jalink|Sakurajima|Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{jalink|Jingo Keiun|Q1373049}}2 ({{jalink|768|Q29961}}). {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{jalink|Soo District|Q1043870|囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{jalink|Kenji|Q1327993}} ({{jalink|1276|Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{jalink|gokenin|Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{jalink|1843|Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{jalink|September 29th|Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Ootoshigami||Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}22 ({{jalink|1947|Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{jalink|Houki|Q18520676}} of {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}} ({{jalink| It dates back to 778 | Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} years ({{jalink|729|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749|Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}} Kushijima, {{jalink| Osumi Province|Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307}} December 8 ({{jalink|764|Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{jalink|766|Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{jalink|Hoki|Q18520676}}9th year ({{jalink|778th year|Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{jalink|Sakurajima|Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{jalink|Jingo Keiun|Q1373049}}2 ({{jalink|768|Q29961}}). {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{jalink|Soo District|Q1043870|囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{jalink|Kenji|Q1327993}} ({{jalink|1276|Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{jalink|gokenin|Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{jalink|1843|Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/855}} "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Part 2"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection) Reference frames 855-856. * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchi Shrine Research Group|author=Shigehisa Fujii|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|year=1978|title=Shikiuchi Shrine Investigation Report Volume 24|publisher={{jalink|Kogakukan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 47 Place names in Kagoshima Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910548|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}}} === External link === Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōsumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] category (P373) == * Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '鹿児島県霧島市国分広瀬3-1089', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} [[JA:大穴持神社 (霧島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{jalink|September 29th|Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Ootoshigami||Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}22 ({{jalink|1947|Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{jalink|Houki|Q18520676}} of {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}} ({{jalink| It dates back to 778 | Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} years ({{jalink|729|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749|Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}} Kushijima, {{jalink| Osumi Province|Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307}} December 8 ({{jalink|764|Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{jalink|766|Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{jalink|Hoki|Q18520676}}9th year ({{jalink|778th year|Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{jalink|Sakurajima|Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{jalink|Jingo Keiun|Q1373049}}2 ({{jalink|768|Q29961}}). {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{jalink|Soo District|Q1043870|囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{jalink|Kenji|Q1327993}} ({{jalink|1276|Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{jalink|gokenin|Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{jalink|1843|Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 47 Place names in Kagoshima Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910548|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}}} === External link === Torii and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{jalink|September 29th|Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Ootoshigami||Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}22 ({{jalink|1947|Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{jalink|Houki|Q18520676}} of {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}} ({{jalink| It dates back to 778 | Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} years ({{jalink|729|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749|Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}} Kushijima, {{jalink| Osumi Province|Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307}} December 8 ({{jalink|764|Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{jalink|766|Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{jalink|Hoki|Q18520676}}9th year ({{jalink|778th year|Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{jalink|Sakurajima|Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{jalink|Jingo Keiun|Q1373049}}2 ({{jalink|768|Q29961}}). {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{jalink|Soo District|Q1043870|囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{jalink|Kenji|Q1327993}} ({{jalink|1276|Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{jalink|gokenin|Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{jalink|1843|Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes Shrine|title={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/855}} "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Part 2"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection) Reference frames 855-856. * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchi Shrine Research Group|author=Shigehisa Fujii|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|year=1978|title=Shikiuchi Shrine Investigation Report Volume 24|publisher={{jalink|Kogakukan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 47 Place names in Kagoshima Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582910548|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}}} === External link === {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{jalink|September 29th|Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Kirishima City|Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{jalink|Ootoshigami||Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}22 ({{jalink|1947|Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{jalink|Houki|Q18520676}} of {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}} ({{jalink| It dates back to 778 | Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}'' {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{jalink|Tenpyo|Q1199879}} years ({{jalink|729|Q23561|729}}-{{jalink|749|Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{jalink|Hyuga Province|Q513737}} Kushijima, {{jalink| Osumi Province|Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307}} December 8 ({{jalink|764|Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. * {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{jalink|766|Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{jalink|Hoki|Q18520676}}9th year ({{jalink|778th year|Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{jalink|Sakurajima|Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{jalink|Jingo Keiun|Q1373049}}2 ({{jalink|768|Q29961}}). {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{jalink|Soo District|Q1043870|囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{jalink|Kenji|Q1327993}} ({{jalink|1276|Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{jalink|gokenin|Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{jalink|Tenpo|Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{jalink|1843|Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes
