{{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill||lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=September 29th|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill||lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Ootoshigami||WD=|lt=Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}22 ({{ill|lt=1947|Q5263|WD=Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Houki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} of {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} ({{ill|lt=It dates back to 778|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} years ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|WD=Q23561|lt=729}}-{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|WD=Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{ill|lt=Hyuga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} Kushijima, {{ill|lt=Osumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} December 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}}9th year ({{ill|lt=778th year|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{ill|lt=Sakurajima|Q128841|WD=Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}}2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961}}). {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|WD=Q1106493 2|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{ill|lt=Soo District|Q1043870|WD=Q1043870|lt=囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{ill|lt=Kenji|Q1327993|WD=Q1327993}} ({{ill|lt=1276|Q5530|WD=Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{ill|lt=gokenin|Q63765|WD=Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|WD=Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place category (P373) == * Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '鹿児島県霧島市国分広瀬3-1089', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} [[JA:大穴持神社 (霧島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=September 29th|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Ootoshigami||WD=|lt=Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}22 ({{ill|lt=1947|Q5263|WD=Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Houki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} of {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} ({{ill|lt=It dates back to 778|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} years ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|WD=Q23561|lt=729}}-{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|WD=Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{ill|lt=Hyuga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} Kushijima, {{ill|lt=Osumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} December 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}}9th year ({{ill|lt=778th year|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{ill|lt=Sakurajima|Q128841|WD=Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}}2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961}}). {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|WD=Q1106493 2|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{ill|lt=Soo District|Q1043870|WD=Q1043870|lt=囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{ill|lt=Kenji|Q1327993|WD=Q1327993}} ({{ill|lt=1276|Q5530|WD=Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{ill|lt=gokenin|Q63765|WD=Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|WD=Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place It is located in [[Ōsumi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická Torii and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)

The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Ootoshigami||WD=|lt=Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}22 ({{ill|lt=1947|Q5263|WD=Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Houki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} of {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} ({{ill|lt=It dates back to 778|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} years ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|WD=Q23561|lt=729}}-{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|WD=Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{ill|lt=Hyuga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} Kushijima, {{ill|lt=Osumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} December 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}}9th year ({{ill|lt=778th year|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{ill|lt=Sakurajima|Q128841|WD=Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}}2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961}}). {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|WD=Q1106493 2|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{ill|lt=Soo District|Q1043870|WD=Q1043870|lt=囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{ill|lt=Kenji|Q1327993|WD=Q1327993}} ({{ill|lt=1276|Q5530|WD=Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{ill|lt=gokenin|Q63765|WD=Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|WD=Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=September 29th|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Ootoshigami|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}22 ({{ill|lt=1947|Q5263|WD=Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Houki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} of {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} ({{ill|lt=It dates back to 778|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} years ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|WD=Q23561|lt=729}}-{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|WD=Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{ill|lt=Hyuga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} Kushijima, {{ill|lt=Osumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} December 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}}9th year ({{ill|lt=778th year|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{ill|lt=Sakurajima|Q128841|WD=Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}}2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961}}). {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|WD=Q1106493 2|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{ill|lt=Soo District|Q1043870|WD=Q1043870|lt=囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{ill|lt=Kenji|Q1327993|WD=Q1327993}} ({{ill|lt=1276|Q5530|WD=Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{ill|lt=gokenin|Q63765|WD=Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|WD=Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1998|chapter=Oanamochi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place Torii and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=September 29th|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Ootoshigami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}22 ({{ill|lt=1947|Q5263|WD=Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Houki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} of {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} ({{ill|lt=It dates back to 778|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} years ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|WD=Q23561|lt=729}}-{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|WD=Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{ill|lt=Hyuga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} Kushijima, {{ill|lt=Osumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} December 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}}9th year ({{ill|lt=778th year|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{ill|lt=Sakurajima|Q128841|WD=Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}}2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961}}). {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|WD=Q1106493 2|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{ill|lt=Soo District|Q1043870|WD=Q1043870|lt=囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{ill|lt=Kenji|Q1327993|WD=Q1327993}} ({{ill|lt=1276|Q5530|WD=Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{ill|lt=gokenin|Q63765|WD=Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|WD=Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=September 29th|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Ootoshigami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}22 ({{ill|lt=1947|Q5263|WD=Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Houki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} of {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} ({{ill|lt=It dates back to 778|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} years ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|WD=Q23561|lt=729}}-{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|WD=Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{ill|lt=Hyuga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} Kushijima, {{ill|lt=Osumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} December 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}}9th year ({{ill|lt=778th year|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{ill|lt=Sakurajima|Q128841|WD=Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}}2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961}}). {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|WD=Q1106493 2|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{ill|lt=Soo District|Q1043870|WD=Q1043870|lt=囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{ill|lt=Kenji|Q1327993|WD=Q1327993}} ({{ill|lt=1276|Q5530|WD=Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{ill|lt=gokenin|Q63765|WD=Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|WD=Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes category (P373) == * Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '鹿児島県霧島市国分広瀬3-1089', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima)}} [[JA:大穴持神社 (霧島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q55540317}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Oanamochi Shrine |Image = [[File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg|250px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=September 29th|Q2882|WD=Q2882}} |Main shrine = |Fudasho, etc. = |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Oanamochi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Oanamochi Shrine'' Oanamochi Shrine] (Kagoshima Prefectural Shrine Agency) is located in {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}} in Kokubun Hirose. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Ootoshigami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}22 ({{ill|lt=1947|Q5263|WD=Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Houki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} of {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} ({{ill|lt=It dates back to 778|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} years ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|WD=Q23561|lt=729}}-{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|WD=Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{ill|lt=Hyuga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} Kushijima, {{ill|lt=Osumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} December 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}}9th year ({{ill|lt=778th year|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{ill|lt=Sakurajima|Q128841|WD=Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}}2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961}}). {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|WD=Q1106493 2|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{ill|lt=Soo District|Q1043870|WD=Q1043870|lt=囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{ill|lt=Kenji|Q1327993|WD=Q1327993}} ({{ill|lt=1276|Q5530|WD=Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{ill|lt=gokenin|Q63765|WD=Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|WD=Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes {{Expand Japanese|大穴持神社 (霧島市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ōmunaji Shrine | native_name = 大穴持神社 | image = Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), entrance.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ōmunaji Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|31.715161|130.756623}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmunaji Shrine'''|大穴持神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Torii and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Kirishima City|Q858352|WD=Q858352}}Kokubu Hirose 3-1089 |Location = {{Coord|31|42|55.27|N|130|45|24.47|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Oanamochi Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Omi Takamiko}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)

The divine emblem is "57 Paulownia" . Commonly known as "Onanjisaa" {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Enshrined God === The current deities enshrined are as follows. Explanation board in the precincts (installed by Kirishima City Board of Education). . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''{{ill|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Oomi Takamikoto}}''' (Onanji no Mikoto/Onamuchi no Mikoto, Okuninushi no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin :* {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona Mikoto}} :* {{ill|lt=Ootoshigami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ootoshigami}} (Otoshigami, Ootoshigami) :* {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sankami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Okami}} :* Kirishima Okami - Moved and enshrined in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}22 ({{ill|lt=1947|Q5263|WD=Q5263}} from the current site of Hirose Community Center).The theory that the personal deity of the enshrined deity is compared to Onamuchi (Ookuninushi) is based on the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Houki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} of {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} ({{ill|lt=It dates back to 778|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}, and it can be seen as ``Oanamochi Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}. It is sometimes introduced as a shrine that shows that the Okuninushi faith, which originated in the Izumo region, extends to Kagoshima.[https://www.nhk.or.jp/special/detail/20140102.html NHK Special "Series Sengu Part 2 Izumo Taisha - The Mystery of the Okuninushi"] NHK, first broadcast on January 2, 2014. . Currently, he is worshiped as a god of medicine and a god of vipers. === History === === Founding === Its founding date is unknown. According to the company records, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyo|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} years ({{ill|lt=729|Q23561|WD=Q23561|lt=729}}-{{ill|lt=749|Q23624|WD=Q23624}}) {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Articles|1912}}. It was originally enshrined on a divine island (Miyazu, Miyazu), which will be mentioned later, but there is a legend that it was relocated to its current location due to the collapse of the island. In "Kokubu Shokoki" and "Shrines and Temples", it is said that it was originally enshrined in Mt. Tsugaru, Oshu, {{ill|lt=Hyuga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} Kushijima, {{ill|lt=Osumi Province|Q981002|WD=Q981002}}Fukusenowatari passed through Fukushima Village and was enshrined here in Komura.{{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. === Outline === In "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}", the ancient Oanamochi Shrine appears as follows {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} December 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}}) Koretsukijo - Three islands appeared due to an eruption that occurred at the border between Osumi Province and {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564}}) June 5th article - ``Osumi no Kuni no Kami'' built Niijima and the tremors continued, and many of the people were exiled, so he donated things and saved them. * {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}}9th year ({{ill|lt=778th year|Q30452|WD=Q30452}}) December 12th article - The name of the god who created the island during the Jingo era was ``Oanamochigami,'' and it was made into a government shrine. The prevailing theory was that the three islands that appeared were Heda-kojima, Benten-jima, and Oki-kojima off the coast of Hayato Port {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. However, volcanically, it is said that they were not caused by eruptions during the Nara period, and there is a theory that the three islands were actually located near {{ill|lt=Sakurajima|Q128841|WD=Q128841}}, but were later submerged under the sea {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. It is also said that this divine island (Miyasu, Miyazu) has a rapid called Miyase on the left side of the shore of the Amori River, so it may have been submerged under water and became the current rapid.In addition to this, the ``''Hachiman Usamiya Imperial Trust Senshu'''' states that an island created under the sea in Osumi due to volcanic activity was named ``Kagoshima'' in {{ill|lt=Jingo Keiun|Q1373049|WD=Q1373049}}2 ({{ill|lt=768|Q29961|WD=Q29961}}). {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q1106493 2|WD=Q1106493 2|lt=In the Shinmeicho}}, ``Ōanamochi Shrine'' is listed in Osumi Province {{ill|lt=Soo District|Q1043870|WD=Q1043870|lt=囎唹 District}}, and it is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1978}}. Regarding the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} period, the ``Ishizuki Jiyaku Shosha'' dated 2nd year {{ill|lt=Kenji|Q1327993|WD=Q1327993}} ({{ill|lt=1276|Q5530|WD=Q5530}}) states that ``Great It is written as ``Anamochi Nitta Gotan'', and it appears that {{ill|lt=gokenin|Q63765|WD=Q63765}} ``Princess Tayu Atsuki (□ is omitted)'' served as the chief priest.{{Sfn|Ōanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}.In the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} period, 3 to 3 sho 3 go {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}} (or 3 to 5 sho 5 go {{ Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Fee|1912}}), and the Taniguchi family has been in charge of the shrine family from that time to the present {{Sfn|Oanamochi Shrine (Heibonsha)|1998}}. In addition, ``{{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}'' 14th year ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|WD=Q7629}}) depicts the precincts at that time, and it is known that the sea stretched out in front of the torii gate. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, May 4th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === Precincts === File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), honden.jpg|Main shrine File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), shaden.jpg|Haden of worship File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), torii.jpg|Torii === Setsusha === There are the following five shrines within the precincts . *Nittengu Shrine * Inari Shrine * Moon Heaven Palace * Otamiya * Great Royal Palace These five shrines are also depicted in the "Sangoku Scenic Spots Zue" and are enshrined in almost the same location as they are today . File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-1.jpg|Shinuchisha File:Onamuji-jinja (Kirishima), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine === Festival === {{hidden begin |title = List of annual festivals |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; ; background:lightgrey; }} List of festivals held annually at Ohanamochi Shrine . * Every morning ** Nichibokusai * Monthly ** Monthly Festival (1st of every month) * January ** Saitan Festival (January 1st) *February ** National Foundation Day (February 11th) * March ** Prayer Festival (February 13th of the lunar calendar) **Vernal Equinox Festival (Vernal Equinox Day) *April ** Viper Exorcism Festival (March 13th of the lunar calendar) * June ** Great Purification (June 30th) * July ** Shinko Festival (July auspicious day) ** June Light Festival (July Auspicious Day) *September **Autumn Equinox Festival (Autumn Equinox Day) ** '''Reitaisai'' (September 29th) *November ** Kirishima Grand Festival (November 19th) ** Labor Thanksgiving Day (November 23rd) *December ** Shimotsuki Ox Festival (formerly November Ox Day) ** Tencho Festival (December 23rd) ** New Year's Eve Festival (Oharai) (December 31st) {{hidden end}} === Footnotes
